excerpt: The Storm have hung on to win a thriller 16-14 in Melbourne against a brave Bulldogs outfit in a match that featured a hat-trick and two sin bins. Canterbury winger Josh Addo-Carr scored three tries in 14 minutes in the second half to give his side the lead, but the hosts found a way back at the death. Jahrome Hughes danced his way through some tired defenders and on the next play Shawn Blore crashed over to score the winner and just his second NRL try. Nick Meaney slotted the 76th minute conversion to put Melbourne ahead and they hung on to make it 13 straight wins at home. title: NRL 2024: Dolphins beat South Sydney Rabbitohs, score, stats, news, results, Tevita Pangai Junior, Isaiya Katoa, Trai Fuller
description: TPJ runs riot in NRL statement; Dolphins half’s stocks rising by day: What we learned
excerpt: South Sydney’s five-game win streak came to a thudding halt on Thursday, with the Dolphins claiming a gripping 36-28 win in Redcliffe as both teams scored six tries apiece. Tevita Pangai Junior delivered his best performance since returning to the NRL from his brief foray into boxing, his physicality and aggression from the opening whistle setting the tone for a much-needed victory. Pangai Junior tormented the Souths defence with his hard running and equally imposing work on defence, the 117kg star scoring a try in a damaging first-half blitz that secured an 18-10 lead at the interval. Pangai ran for well over 100 metres, busting through tackles with ease, and set up a crucial late try that staved off a second-half Souths charge. Desperate to keep their win streak alive, South Sydney fought until the death but were laid low by poor goal-kicking from Cody Walker and Taane Milne, the pair combining for just two of six successful conversion attempts. After losing four of the last five games, the Dolphins were clearly fired up on a night Kenny Bromwich celebrated his 250th NRL appearance.
